data.title: Keir Starmer Faces Challenges Amid Iran Conflict and Inflation
data.description: Keir Starmer is under significant pressure as he navigates the complexities of the Iran conflict, rising inflation, and internal dissent. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the mounting challenges faced by Starmer, who is also dealing with a potential leadership challenge from a favored contender. The situation underscores the multifaceted issues confronting Starmer in his current role.

data.title: Whale account "tummy.hl" has opened a long position in HYPE with 3x leverage, currently valued at approximately $5.33 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, whale "tummy.hl" has opened a 3x leveraged long position in HYPE, currently holding approximately 135,065.05 HYPE tokens, worth approximately $5.33 million, and may further increase its position in the future.

data.title: French Woman Dies from Invasive Meningococcal Disease
data.description:
A woman in France has died from invasive meningococcal disease, with local health authorities stating that there is currently no connection between this case and the outbreak in the United Kingdom. According to RTHK, the woman was employed at a factory in the Normandy region. The company has identified approximately 50 individuals who may have been in contact with her. These individuals will undergo a 48-hour preventive antibiotic treatment and will be monitored at home for 10 days.

Recently, an outbreak of invasive meningococcal disease has been reported in Kent, United Kingdom, with at least 29 cases and two fatalities. The situation in France remains under investigation as health officials work to prevent further spread of the disease.

data.title: Trader Bets on U.S. Military Action in Iran by March 31
data.description: A trader has created a new wallet named 'mzandres' and invested $26,000 in a prediction that the U.S. military will enter Iran by March 31. According to Odaily, this move was detected by Lookonchian monitoring. The prediction reflects a significant financial commitment based on geopolitical developments.

data.title: A trader created a new wallet named "mzandres" and deposited $26,000, predicting that US troops would enter Iran before the end of March.
data.description: According to Lookonchian's monitoring, a trader created a new wallet "mzandres" and then invested $26,000 to predict that US troops would enter Iran before March 31.

data.title: An Ethereum OG (Original Expert) spent $3 million to buy 1,401 ETH, bringing his total ETH holdings this week to nearly $20 million.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, Ethereum OG "thomasg.eth" spent approximately $3 million to acquire about 1,401 ETH about 15 hours ago, bringing its total ETH purchases this week to $19.5 million. It is understood that thomasg.eth once held ETH, WBTC, and DAI worth as much as $538 million at the market peak in 2021.

data.title: Musk suffers rare legal defeat: found liable for "misleading investors" during Twitter acquisition.
data.description: A U.S. federal court in San Francisco has found Elon Musk liable for intentionally driving down Twitter's (now renamed Twitter X) stock price during his 2022 acquisition of the company, thus "misleading investors." However, the jury dismissed the fraud charge that Musk "conspired" to mislead investors. The lawsuit, filed before Musk took over Twitter, required the jury to determine whether two tweets Musk posted in May 2022, and his remarks on a related podcast, constituted "intentional fraud against Twitter shareholders." The nine-member jury, after nearly four days of deliberation, ruled that Musk was responsible for "misleading investors" with the two tweets, but his remarks on the podcast were not misleading, and he did not intentionally "conspire" to defraud investors. This verdict is a rare defeat for Musk, and his legal team has indicated they will appeal. Musk announced his $44 billion acquisition of Twitter in April 2022 and terminated the agreement on July 8. Twitter sued Musk on July 12, accusing him of refusing to fulfill his contractual acquisition obligations, causing Twitter significant losses. (Reuters)

data.title: AI TRENDS | Apple CEO Discusses Mac Mini's Role in AI Tasks
data.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook recently addressed the surge in Mac Mini sales driven by OpenClaw during his visit to China. According to Odaily, Cook highlighted that Apple integrated neural network engines into Macs a decade ago. He emphasized that the combination of Apple chips, generative AI, and intelligent interaction has made the Mac Mini an ideal computer for AI tasks. Apple plans to continue optimizing Mac performance for AI workflows, noting that users can already train large language models on MacBook Pro.

data.title: Cook responds to OpenClaw's contribution to Mac Mini sales: Neural Engine was added ten years ago
data.description: During a Mac-related trip to China, Apple CEO Tim Cook addressed the recent surge in Mac Mini sales driven by OpenClaw in an interview. He commented that Apple incorporated the Neural Engine into Macs a decade ago, and with the combined development of Apple chips, generative AI, and intelligent agent interaction, this hardware and software integration makes the Mac Mini the most suitable computer for AI tasks. Apple will continue to optimize Mac performance for AI workflows; in fact, users can already train large language models on the Macbook Pro. (Jiemian)

data.title: EU Energy Commissioner Suggests Lower Gas Storage Targets
data.description: The European Union's Energy Commissioner, Jorgensen, has proposed that EU member states reduce their gas storage targets to 80% of capacity. According to Jin10, this recommendation is 10 percentage points lower than the official EU target. The suggestion comes amid ongoing discussions about energy security and supply within the region.

data.title: China Encourages Eli Lilly to Expand Commitment for Growth
data.description: China's Commerce Minister Wang Wentao expressed his hope that Eli Lilly will enhance its engagement in the country and pursue further growth opportunities. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the statement released on the ministry's website. Wang's remarks underscore China's interest in fostering stronger ties with international companies to boost economic development.

data.title: Bitdeer Reports Zero Bitcoin Holdings Amid Mining Operations
data.description: Bitdeer, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company, has released its latest Bitcoin holdings data on the X platform. According to PANews, as of the week ending March 20, Bitdeer maintained zero Bitcoin holdings. During this period, the company produced 126.3 BTC through mining operations and sold an equivalent amount of 126.3 BTC.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards: Explosions Occur at Bushehr Base
data.description: Two explosions have been reported at a base of Iran's Revolutionary Guards in Bushehr, according to Iranian media. The cause and impact of the explosions remain unclear at this time. Further details are awaited as authorities investigate the incident.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin, along with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq, is under pressure; the Iran war exacerbates market risk aversion.
data.description: Bitcoin fell nearly 5% this week due to the impact of the US-Israel-Iran war, with the S&P 500, Dow Jones, Nasdaq, and gold all declining, while crude oil rose 7.3%, a 53% increase since the war began on February 28. A Kobeissi Letter report stated that the S&P 500 ETF and Nasdaq 100 ETF saw a combined outflow of $64 billion over the past three months, a record high, representing approximately 5% of total assets under management. The spot Bitcoin ETF also recorded a net outflow of $253 million in the past two days. Glassnode data shows that the market struggled to absorb selling pressure, with net profit-taking in Bitcoin accelerating to approximately $17 million per hour at one point, but subsequently losing momentum, and the price fell back below $70,000. Analysts point out that geopolitical uncertainty has compressed market demand depth, making even a medium-sized sell-off difficult to absorb. Historical experience shows that Bitcoin's price action is repeating itself, similar to that during the Russia-Ukraine war: an initial sell-off followed by a short-term rebound, but then continued downward pressure. Analysts believe that rising energy costs, tight liquidity, and continued pressure from forced selling will make Bitcoin's recovery take longer. Finish predicts that Bitcoin may bottom out around $55,000 before gradually rebounding, but the market remains cautious until the conflict with Iran subsides. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Ship Adopts Identity of Scrapped Carrier Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: A vessel, seemingly assuming the identity of a decommissioned gas carrier, has recently navigated through the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the evolving tactics employed to traverse this critical waterway as the conflict in the Middle East intensifies. The incident underscores the complexities and challenges faced by maritime operations in the region, where geopolitical tensions continue to influence shipping routes and strategies.

data.title: Analysts: MSTR buys in to ease pressure after Bitcoin whales sell off their holdings; market sentiment remains fragile.
data.description: Crypto analyst Murphy published an article on the X platform reviewing the market dynamics of the past week, revealing unusual Bitcoin fund flows: On March 11th and 13th, when BTC rebounded to $70,000–$71,000, a group of whale addresses (each holding over 1,000 BTC) sold 24,867 and 17,818 BTC respectively. The shares sold on March 11th had an average loss of 50% compared to cost, while the shares sold on March 13th had a loss of less than 10%. Analysis shows that these shares originated from purchases made at low prices in May–July 2025 and early April, with some exiting after significant losses and others being rotated out after precise bottom-fishing. Strategy bought another 22,337 BTC on March 17th, absorbing most of the excess supply. Nevertheless, this event reflects that market sentiment remains extremely fragile, and the patience of investors who have held BTC for nearly a year is gradually wearing thin. Future price rebounds may still trigger pressure from those holding losing positions.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs Experience Consecutive Net Outflows
data.description: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded a net outflow of $52.1092 million on March 20, extending the streak to three consecutive days. According to NS3.AI, US spot Ethereum ETFs also experienced a third straight day of net outflows, amounting to $41.9715 million.

data.title: Greek Cargo Ship Enters Persian Gulf with AIS Active, Reports Say
data.description: A Greek bulk carrier, identified as the 'Giacometti,' has reportedly entered the Persian Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz with its Automatic Identification System (AIS) active, marking the first such occurrence since March 2. According to Jin10, the vessel, flying the Liberian flag, is owned by a Greek bulk shipping company. The AIS data indicates that the ship is transporting food cargo destined for Iran. On March 20, local time, the ship navigated around Larak Island, following a route similar to the 'safe corridor' delineated by Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

data.title: Bitdeer currently maintains zero holdings and sold 126.3 BTC this week.
data.description: Bitdeer, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company, released its latest Bitcoin holdings data on the X platform. It still maintains zero Bitcoin holdings. As of the week ending March 20, its Bitcoin mining output was 126.3 BTC, but it sold 126.3 BTC during the same period.
